heading: Duties of employees of Capitol Guide Service
label: 2242 Duties of employees of Capitol Guide Service

children.heading: Tours
children.label: 1 Tours
children.text: In accordance with this section, the Capitol Guide Service shall provide without charge guided tours of the interior of the United States Capitol, including the Capitol Visitor Center, for the education and enlightenment of the general public.

children.heading: Acceptance of fees prohibited
children.label: 2 Acceptance of fees prohibited
children.text: An employee of the Capitol Guide Service shall not charge or accept any fee, or accept any gratuity, for or on account of the official services of that employee.

children.heading: Regulations of the Architect of the Capitol
children.label: 3 Regulations of the Architect of the Capitol
children.text: All such tours shall be conducted in compliance with regulations approved by the Architect of the Capitol, upon recommendation of the Chief Executive Officer.

children.heading: Authority of the Architect of the Capitol
children.label: b Authority of the Architect of the Capitol

children.text: subject to the availability of appropriations, establish and revise such number of positions of Guide in the Capitol Guide Service as the Architect of the Capitol considers necessary to carry out effectively the activities of the Capitol Guide Service;

appoint, on a permanent basis without regard to political affiliation and solely on the basis of fitness to perform their duties, a Chief Guide and such deputies as the Architect of the Capitol considers appropriate for the effective administration of the Capitol Guide Service and, in addition, such number of Guides as may be authorized;

children.label: A
children.text: prescribe the individual’s duties and responsibilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: fix, and adjust from time to time, respective rates of pay at single per annum (gross) rates;

with respect to the individuals appointed under paragraph (2), take appropriate disciplinary action, including, when circumstances warrant, suspension from duty without pay, reduction in pay, demotion, or termination of employment with the Capitol Guide Service, against any employee who violates any provision of this section or any regulation prescribed by the Architect of the Capitol under paragraph (8);
prescribe a uniform dress, including appropriate insignia, which shall be worn by personnel of the Capitol Guide Service;
from time to time and as may be necessary, procure and furnish such uniforms to such personnel without charge to such personnel;

receive and consider advice and information from any private historical or educational organization, association, or society with respect to those operations of the Capitol Guide Service which involve the furnishing of historical and educational information to the general public; and

with the approval of the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate and the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives, prescribe such regulations as the Architect of the Capitol considers necessary and appropriate for the operation of the Capitol Guide Service, including regulations with respect to tour routes and hours of operation, number of visitors per guide, staff-led tours, and non-law enforcement security and special event related support.

children.heading: Provision of accessible tours in coordination with Office of Congressional Accessibility Services
children.label: c Provision of accessible tours in coordination with Office of Congressional Accessibility Services
children.text: The Chief Executive Officer shall coordinate the provision of accessible tours for individuals with disabilities with the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services established under part B.
the inauguration of the President and Vice President of the United States;
the official reception of representatives of foreign nations and other persons by the Senate or House of Representatives; or

children.label: A
children.text: require the presence of additional Government personnel; and

children.label: B
children.text: cause the temporary suspension of the performance of regular duties.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on the transfer date.
